banco_oraciones.oracion: I DO understand why you're upset.
She DOES care about this project, even if it doesn't look like it.
It was such a good day that nobody wanted to leave.
The exam was so difficult that half the class failed.
It wasn't such a bad idea after all.
The traffic wasn't so bad that we missed the flight.
It didn't seem such a big deal at the time.
Do you really understand, or are you just saying that?
Was it such a good day that you didn't want to leave?
Did the exam really matter that much to you?
Do you actually understand? I DO understand, yes.
Was the exam that hard? Yes, it was so hard that half the class failed.
Did you enjoy the day? I DID enjoy it, actually.

interaccion: Kenji: You never seem to care about the results.
Sofía: I **DO care**, Kenji. I just don't panic out loud like you do.
Ben: It was **such a** stressful week that I actually finished something early, for once.
Abuela Carmen: The pressure **DID do** something good, then. Write that down before it disappears.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I really understand why you're upset.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I DO understand why you're upset.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Adding do/does/did before the base verb, or using so/such before an adjective or noun, adds emphasis — it insists on something that might be doubted.

filas: It was so difficult that everyone struggled.
It was such a good day that nobody left.

ejercicios.pregunta: It was ___ good day that nobody wanted to leave.
The exam was ___ difficult that half the class failed.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'I DO understand' is more emphatic than 'I understand'.
'So' is used directly before a noun (so a good day).
'Such' is used before an adjective + noun combination (such a good day).

ejercicios.texto: She ___ care about this project, even if it doesn't show.
The traffic wasn't ___ bad that we missed the flight.
It wasn't ___ bad idea after all.

ejercicios.respuesta: I DO understand why you're upset
it was such a good day that nobody left

ejercicios.texto_con_error: It was so good day that nobody wanted to leave.
ejercicios.respuesta: It was such a good day that nobody wanted to leave.

ejercicios.instruccion: Rewrite with emphatic stress using DO.

ejercicios.oracion_base: The exam was very difficult; half the class failed.
ejercicios.respuesta: The exam was so difficult that half the class failed.

ejercicios.oracion_base: It was a very good day; nobody wanted to leave.
ejercicios.respuesta: It was such a good day that nobody wanted to leave.

ejercicios.oracion_base: It was such a bad idea.
ejercicios.instruccion: Write a version meaning the opposite (it wasn't bad).
ejercicios.respuesta: It wasn't such a bad idea after all.

ejercicios.pregunta: Do you really enjoy studying grammar? (answer with emphasis using DO)

guion: Kenji: You never seem to care about the results.
Sofía: I DO care, Kenji. I just don't panic out loud like you do.
Ben: It was such a stressful week that I actually finished something early, for once.
Kenji: That IS surprising, coming from you.
Sofía: The pressure did do something good, then.
Ben: Don't get used to it. It was such a rare event.

comprension.pregunta: Does Sofía care about the results?
opciones: Yes, she does, even if it doesn't show
comprension.respuesta: Yes, she does, even if it doesn't show

comprension.pregunta: What happened because of the stressful week, according to Ben?

reading.texto: Kenji has always assumed Sofía doesn't worry about deadlines — she never raises her voice, never seems rattled. 'I DO worry,' she told him once, 'I just don't show it the way you do.' It was such a strange thing to hear that Kenji actually stopped and reconsidered everything he thought he knew about her. The week that followed was so demanding that even Sofía's calm cracked a little — she DID admit, finally, that it had been hard. Ben, of all people, finished his part early that week, which was such a rare event that Abuela Carmen wrote the date down, just so nobody would forget it happened.

preguntas.afirmacion: Sofía told Kenji she doesn't worry about deadlines.
Kenji reconsidered what he thought about Sofía.
Sofía admitted the week had been hard.
Ben finishing early was a common occurrence.
